cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The state in which the forward and reverse reaction rates are equal and concentrations remain constant
cards.answer: Chemical equilibrium

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: For $a\text{A} + b\text{B} \rightleftharpoons c\text{C} + d\text{D}$: $K_c = \frac{[\text{C}]^c[\text{D}]^d}{[\text{A}]^a[\text{B}]^b}$
cards.answer: Equilibrium constant expression ($K_c$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $K_p = K_c(RT)^{\Delta n}$ where $\Delta n$ = moles gas products $-$ moles gas reactants
cards.answer: Relationship between $K_p$ and $K_c$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Le Chatelier's principle
cards.answer: If a stress is applied to a system at equilibrium, the system shifts to partially relieve that stress

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Adding more product to a reaction at equilibrium causes the equilibrium to shift...
cards.answer: Left (toward reactants), to consume the added product

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Increasing temperature for an endothermic reaction ($\Delta H > 0$) shifts equilibrium...
cards.answer: Right (toward products), because heat is a reactant; $K_c$ increases

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Increasing pressure by decreasing volume shifts equilibrium toward...
cards.answer: The side with fewer moles of gas

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The ratio of product concentrations to reactant concentrations at any point in time (not just at equilibrium)
cards.answer: Reaction quotient

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: If $Q < K$, the reaction proceeds in which direction to reach equilibrium?
cards.answer: Forward (toward products), because there are too few products relative to equilibrium

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: If $Q > K$, the reaction proceeds in which direction to reach equilibrium?
cards.answer: Reverse (toward reactants), because there are too many products relative to equilibrium

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A systematic table used to calculate equilibrium concentrations: Initial, Change, Equilibrium
cards.answer: ICE table

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Pure solids and pure liquids are excluded from equilibrium constant expressions because their activity = 1
cards.answer: Activity of pure solids/liquids

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $K_{sp} = [\text{M}^{m+}]^m[\text{X}^{n-}]^n$ for the dissolution $\text{M}_n\text{X}_m\text{(s)} \rightleftharpoons m\text{M}^{n+}\text{(aq)} + n\text{X}^{m-}\text{(aq)}$
cards.answer: Solubility product expression ($K_{sp}$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The reduction in solubility of a salt when a common ion is added to the solution
cards.answer: Common ion effect

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta G^\circ = -RT\ln K$ relating standard free energy to the equilibrium constant
cards.answer: Relationship between $\Delta G^\circ$ and $K$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: If $K \gg 1$ (very large), the equilibrium lies far to the...
cards.answer: Right (products side); essentially goes to completion

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: If $K \ll 1$ (very small), the equilibrium lies far to the...
cards.answer: Left (reactants side); very little product forms

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: When equilibrium constant for reaction 2 is the reciprocal of reaction 1 ($K_2 = 1/K_1$)
cards.answer: Reverse reaction relationship

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: When two reactions are added, their equilibrium constants are multiplied: $K_{\text{total}} = K_1 \times K_2$
cards.answer: Multiplying equilibrium constants for coupled reactions

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Adding an inert gas at constant volume to a gas-phase equilibrium causes the equilibrium to...
cards.answer: Not shift; concentrations and partial pressures of reactants/products are unchanged
